rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA YEE LIN MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service is incomplete at item 2. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Was notice of these proceedings given to the Canadian consulate? [Probate Code Section 8113.] (4) Detailed schedules supporting an accounting must show the source of receipts or payee of disbursements and sufficient descriptions of each entry. [Probate Code Section 1062(a) and (b).] (5) Both the Bank of America and Capital One accounts appear to be in the executor's individual name, not the estate's. (Fully redacting the account number instead of all but the last four digits is not helpful.) (6) Any difference between the statement(s) and the account must be reconciled. [LRSF 14.35 D.] (7) One-half of $3,469.44 is $1,734.72. (8) Receipts in the first account included a refund of $541.97 HOA dues and $7,612.38 advanced by the executor, which are not receipts includable in the fee base. (9) Attorney costs must be set forth in detail, including dates, payees, description, and each amount. (10) Paragraph 26 does not appear to have correct figures either. (11) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete legal description of real property (which must also be part of the proposed order). [CRC 7.651] Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/3/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HIDEO IWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The administrator never petitioned for waiver of a formal appraisal. [Probate Code Sect. 8903.] 2) At least one of the figures is also incorrect: her item 51 is shown at $100, but the Public Guardian's final accounting showed this group of coins, etc. [N004], to be $170. 3) The administrator sold some of the currency and coins to her attorney for $431.00 without obtaining court supervision, as required by Probate Code Section 10501(a)(5). 4) Administrator has maintained well over the FDIC insured amount in one bank account. 5) Her "Verified Supplement," etc., amended the fourth amended account, in particular, increasing the statutory compensation to the attorney and the administrator; the caption is misleading. [CRC 7.3.] 6) Notice was mailed to nephew Takayuki Tsuji at 6-25-11 Shimoshakuji, Apt. 1F-A, etc., but his address is shown in the fourth amended petition to be 6-35-11... 7) The tangible personal property transferred from the Public Guardian totaled $13,120.00, not $12,370.00. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FLORENCE V. TASSANO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/3/2025. The following issues have not been cleared since the last tentative ruling. The issues must be cleared two weeks before the hearing: (1) Written declination to act or consent from the decedent's daughter to the proposed administrator's appointment. Local rule 14.24. (2) Proposed order to be submitted. Local Rule 14.7.A. (3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [Form DE-147S] should not be attached to DE-147 as the form instructs. Local Rule 14.18.E. (4) Proof of service of the notice must be dated by the server in the signature line. (5) Proof of service of Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances. Local Rule 14.1.C.6) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUANITA ELEANOR CHECCHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/9/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (3) The original will does not appear to have been filed yet. (4) The petitioner and the alternate executor are missing from item 8 of the petition. (5) The alternate executor was not given notice. (6) Who is the trustee of the devisee trust? If it is the petitioner, the petition must clearly identify all of the trust beneficiaries, and they must all receive notice. (7) Notice must be given directly to a person entitled to notice, not in care of another. If a party entitled to notice is represented by an attorney, notice must also be given to the attorney, but not only to the attorney. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b)]. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/26/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL WRIGHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/10/2025. Notes emailed to counsel on 01/28/2025.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEFINA CATUDAN RAMOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
